note: Machine-readable export of Policy Matrix v1. Mirrors packages/privacy/src/policyMatrix.ts verbatim (drift is test-enforced). Static version note instead of a timestamp to keep tests stable.

specs.rationale: No implemented persistent backend exists; Standard targets the future encrypted backend and fails hard until Gate F.
Content lives in memory only; Standard content targets the unimplemented encrypted backend (no silent memory fallback); Emergency denies normal writes.
Key material is never storable until the encrypted backend passes Gate F review.
Capsule spool is memory-only active state; nothing persists in v1.
Preview caches are content-adjacent; denied in Private+ and sealed shield; Standard may hold a memory-only cache.
Thumbnails are derived content; same policy as preview caches.
AI caches are content in disguise; denied everywhere until Gate I.
Audit events are redacted (no content/identifiers) and memory-only; Emergency keeps only a redacted wipe/revoke placeholder.
Debug artifacts are denied everywhere in v1.
Protected reveal state never persists; behavioral metadata.
Notification content storage is born denied (recorded TECH-10 decision).
The encrypted persistent backend is a placeholder that throws until Gate F.
Direct HTTP is forbidden until an approved transport exists.
Relay send is a user-initiated approved-transport PLACEHOLDER (no network implemented); strict/offline/emergency deny.
Persistent bidirectional channels are forbidden.
Direct peer connections expose IP (ICE/STUN); denied pending explicit future policy.
LAN discovery broadcasts presence metadata; denied by default.
Remote assets are network side effects; forbidden (ADR-0008).
Automatic link previews leak URL/IP/timing; forbidden.
Telemetry is forbidden in every mode, forever (ADR-0008).
Remote AI requests are forbidden; AI is local-only by design (Gate I).
Automatic update checks are denied; manual/future only.
Push needs a push service + subscription metadata; no infrastructure by default.
Service workers add background network/cache surface; none exists and none is allowed.
Read receipts are relationship metadata; off by default, denied in Private+ (v1 denies all).
Delivery acks must later be sealed capsule content, not transport-visible plaintext.
Typing indicators reveal real-time activity.
Presence/online status reveals availability patterns.
Last-seen is retained availability metadata.
Room-activity signals enable room/contact-graph inference.
Send/receive timing is network metadata; denied in v1 (no network).
Message/capsule size is correlatable metadata; padding is future CapsuleNet work.
Relay choice reveals infrastructure relationships.
Preview existence/URL interest is content-adjacent metadata.
Asset-fetch requests leak IP/timing/interest.
Audit events exist only redacted (numeric/boolean details) and memory-only.
Only non-sensitive, memory-only logs; Emergency denies normal logging.
AI prompt/cache/summary existence is metadata; denied (Gate I).
Capture-detected signals are memory-only and redacted (never plaintext).
Reveal state is memory-only where allowed; strict modes and sealed shield deny it.
Notification previews/badges are activity metadata.
Automatic previews are denied in every mode; one auto-fetch leaks URL/IP/UA/timing.
OpenGraph fetching parses remote pages; denied.
Favicon fetches leak browsing interest.
A preview cache would persist URL/title/image; denied.
URLs render only as redacted plain text (domain-only, no query/credentials); sealed shield collapses to a generic label.
A safe user-initiated preview is a future research/design gate; not implemented.
External asset kind "remote_image" is forbidden in every mode (ADR-0008); assets travel as capsule content, never remote URLs.
External asset kind "remote_avatar" is forbidden in every mode (ADR-0008); assets travel as capsule content, never remote URLs.
External asset kind "remote_font" is forbidden in every mode (ADR-0008); assets travel as capsule content, never remote URLs.
External asset kind "remote_script" is forbidden in every mode (ADR-0008); assets travel as capsule content, never remote URLs.
External asset kind "remote_stylesheet" is forbidden in every mode (ADR-0008); assets travel as capsule content, never remote URLs.
External asset kind "remote_css_url" is forbidden in every mode (ADR-0008); assets travel as capsule content, never remote URLs.
External asset kind "tracking_pixel" is forbidden in every mode (ADR-0008); assets travel as capsule content, never remote URLs.
External asset kind "iframe" is forbidden in every mode (ADR-0008); assets travel as capsule content, never remote URLs.
External asset kind "preconnect" is forbidden in every mode (ADR-0008); assets travel as capsule content, never remote URLs.
External asset kind "dns_prefetch" is forbidden in every mode (ADR-0008); assets travel as capsule content, never remote URLs.
External asset kind "preload" is forbidden in every mode (ADR-0008); assets travel as capsule content, never remote URLs.
External asset kind "prefetch" is forbidden in every mode (ADR-0008); assets travel as capsule content, never remote URLs.
Permission prompts are side effects; never automatic (future explicit user action).
The only allowed notification: a generic, content-free, memory-only in-app indicator (Standard/Private/Sovereign).
Message previews in notifications are denied in every mode.
Room names in notifications leak context; denied.
Sender aliases in notifications leak relationships; denied.
Badge counts are activity metadata; not implemented, denied in strict modes forever.
Even a contentless badge dot reveals activity.
Notification sound reveals timing/activity patterns.
Vibration reveals timing/activity patterns.
Push subscription requires infrastructure + an ADR gate; denied in every mode.
Push receive is denied until the push gate opens (if ever).
Service-worker notifications are denied; no service worker exists.
Notification denials are auditable only as redacted events (no title/body/names).
Local AI is future-gated (Gate I) and denied outright in Ghost/Bunker/Emergency.
Remote AI is forbidden in every mode; AI is local-only by design.
AI prompt caches are denied everywhere in v1.
Embedding indexes are content-derived; denied.
AI output caches are denied.
AI summaries never reach notifications by default.
AI-policy denials are auditable only as redacted events.
Protected reveal is memory-only where allowed; strict modes and sealed shield deny.
Reveal history never persists.
Capture audits are redacted and memory-only; never plaintext.
Watermark/canary state is memory-only and must never contain raw identity/content.
The Clipboard Firewall is TECH-EDL-05; until designed, protected copy is gated.
Secure input firewall is TECH-EDL-06; future-gated.
Task-switcher preview hiding is platform work (TECH-EDL); future-gated.
Screenshot-blocking capability is per-platform and honest-limits work; future-gated.
No cryptography is written or claimed until CRYPTO_DESIGN passes Gate F review.
Capsule wire format + hostile-input parser are Gate E work.
Real capsule transports arrive with Gate D/E; only Noop/Mock exist.
Room sync model (event-log vs CRDT) is Gate H work.
Local room creation is a memory-only state transition; Emergency denies.
Placeholder object mutations (message/note/task/decision/poll/member refs) are memory-only; Emergency denies.
Lifecycle transitions (rename/archive/lock/tombstone) are memory-only; allowed in Emergency for the lock/wipe direction.
Room policy updates compose tighten-only and live in memory.
The event-sourced operation log is a memory-only placeholder in v1.
Operation-log persistence is denied until the encrypted backend exists (Gate F); Ghost/Bunker deny forever.
Materialized projection is derived, rebuildable, memory-only.
Projection persistence is denied until the encrypted backend exists (Gate F).
Room audit events exist only redacted (no titles/member names/content) and memory-only.
Reading the memory-only operation log is a local, policy-gated access.
Clearing the memory log is allowed in every mode (wipe direction, incl. Emergency).
Deterministic replay is a pure, local, in-memory reconstruction of accepted events.
Snapshots/compaction are a future derived-data storage class: they can retain deleted content and depend on Gate F encryption + StoragePolicy/PBOM review.
Creating a local object (message/note/task/decision/poll/file_ref) is a memory-only transition; content never persists; Emergency denies.
Editing local object content/status (explicit commands only; no generic patch) is memory-only; Emergency denies ordinary edits.
Redaction removes local object content (memory-only) and is allowed in every mode incl. Emergency (safe direction).
Archiving makes a local object read-only (memory-only); Emergency denies.
Tombstoning is terminal and content-clearing (memory-only), allowed in every mode incl. Emergency (wipe direction). Not forensic erasure.
Persistent plaintext object content is denied until the encrypted backend exists (Gate F); Ghost/Bunker deny forever.
The object mutation log is a memory-only placeholder; append requires its OWN storage decision, separate from the mutation.
Poll voting/tallies require verified identity (Gate G) and sync (Gate H); no vote capability exists in v1.
Resolving/reading a file reference (bytes) is future work; file refs hold no path/URL/bytes (Gate E capsule design).
File/thumbnail previews are forbidden — no rendering, no fetch, no MIME-driven preview.
Remote file references / fetch / upload / download are forbidden (no network in RoomOS).
AI memory-ref objects carry no AI content/embeddings/model calls; future-gated (Gate I).
Endpoint-hook objects are externalized placeholders — no capture, no monitoring, no active protection; integration requires Gate R.
Room summary query is a side-effect-free local read; strict modes return a redacted summary. Allowed (minimally) even in Emergency.
Object/task/decision/poll/file-ref list queries are local memory-only reads (metadata-minimized summaries); Emergency denies.
Object detail query returns in-memory content only where policy allows (never in Bunker/Emergency); redacted/tombstoned content never reappears.
Exact case-sensitive in-memory substring search only (no index/history/cache/snippet); Bunker/Emergency deny; the term is never logged.
Object counts are metadata behind their OWN scope; Standard/Offline may allow exact local counts, Private/Ghost/Bunker deny by default.
Query history is forbidden in every mode — no query term/kind/result is ever retained.
Query-result caching is forbidden in every mode.
A persistent search/token index would duplicate content; forbidden in v1 (future design gate required).
Semantic/embedding/NL search requires local AI — future-gated (Gate I).
Remote/synchronized queries require the sync model — future-gated (Gate H).
Content-bearing query views in Bunker require a future protected-presentation integration gate; denied in TECH-19.
Bootstrapping the first local owner-placeholder is a memory-only transition during room creation; Emergency denies.
Adding a local placeholder member is memory-only; Bunker/Emergency deny membership expansion.
Role changes are memory-only and preserve last-owner continuity; Bunker/Emergency deny.
Suspension is a restrictive/safety-direction transition (memory-only); may be permitted even in Bunker/Emergency when policy allows. Last active owner cannot be suspended.
Reactivation is membership expansion (memory-only); Bunker/Emergency deny.
Removal tombstones a membership (terminal, memory-only, wipe direction); may be permitted in Bunker/Emergency. Last active owner cannot be removed.
The membership event log is a memory-only placeholder; append needs its OWN storage decision, separate from the mutation.
Redacted membership listing is a local read; Bunker/Emergency deny ordinary listing. Never exposes identity/presence/capability descriptors.
Redacted membership detail is a local read; Bunker/Emergency deny.
Membership count is activity metadata behind its OWN scope; Standard/Offline/Sovereign allow, Private/Ghost/Bunker/Emergency deny.
Capability resolution is a pure, local, side-effect-free policy computation producing a non-authoritative descriptor (never a token).
Capability descriptors are never persisted or cached (they are not credentials).
Capability descriptor serialization is forbidden (no bearer tokens / capability URLs).
Capability delegation is not implemented (a real capability runtime is Gate B).
Invites require identity + capsule wire format — future-gated (Gate G/E).
Identity verification of members is Gate G; every member stays unverified in v1.
Distributed revocation requires the sync model — future-gated (Gate H). Local removal is not distributed revocation.
Endpoint-risk is a local, untrusted policy input that can only TIGHTEN — never identity assurance or authority. Integration is externalized (Gate R).
Preparing an authorization context is a pure, local, side-effect-free computation producing a non-authoritative, transient input (never a token).
Execution-time revalidation re-checks a prepared context against CURRENT state + an authentic exact-scope decision immediately before the side effect.
No authorization allow-result, capability descriptor, or prepared context may be cached or reused without current-revision revalidation.
Prepared authorization contexts are transient and never persisted.
Prepared authorization contexts are never serialized (they are not bearer credentials or transport payloads).
Distributed/broadcast revocation across devices requires the sync model — future-gated (Gate H). Local invalidation is not distributed revocation.
Signed revocations / revocation certificates require crypto + identity — future-gated (Gates F/G).
Endpoint/device-risk state can only TIGHTEN or deny — it can never grant, restore, or prove authority (externalized, Gate R).
Policy composition is a pure, local, deterministic computation using strictest-policy-wins + deny-overrides. No side effects, no content in the result.
Room governance is tighten-only; there is no loosen/reset/enable-forbidden-feature/lower-posture/disable-protection command.

A room governance update (tighten/increase-posture/increase-sensitivity/strengthen-protected/disable-feature) is a memory-only, owner-only, monotonic transition; may run in Emergency (restrictive direction).
The governance event log is a memory-only placeholder; append needs its OWN storage decision, separate from the update.
Effective device posture is resolved locally and fail-closed: unverified by default, at_risk on untrusted tightening; never elevated.
An untrusted/caller signal may reduce trust but may NEVER increase it. No provider is integrated; higher-posture claims are ignored.
Device posture signals + posture history are transient memory-only; persistence is forbidden.
Real device-posture verification (basic/hardened/high_assurance/managed_bunker) is supplied by the SEPARATE Secure Device project — future integration gate + ADR.
Protected-content presentation (Secure Device / ScreenShield) is not integrated; any future-required protection DENIES content display now (no silent downgrade).
Core never claims active capture protection / ScreenShield. `activeProtectionClaim` is structurally false.
Sensitive-room admission is a deterministic local decision gating content by posture + protected presentation; content denies when the provider is absent.
Secure Device provider integration (posture provenance/freshness/anti-replay) is a SEPARATE project + dedicated gate/ADR; not integrated in core.
Distributed governance consensus / synchronized policy revision is Gate H.
Signed/verified room policy + governance authorship require crypto + identity (Gates F/G).
Reading Secure Device provider status is a local, static, content-free lookup; in core it always reports not_integrated and trustedForPostureElevation=false.
Reading provider capabilities is a static content-free lookup; every capability is unavailable/future-gated and raw-evidence/device-management/telemetry are forbidden.
Reading the normalized posture assessment is transient/memory-only; effective posture is unverified (or at_risk); no raw evidence or device identifiers are included.
An untrusted signal may tighten to at_risk (a RESTRICTION only); at_risk overrides every higher claim and never grants or restores access.
Raw attestation Evidence is NEVER accepted as input to core; as the RATS Relying Party, FreeLayer consumes only a normalized result (evidence-bearing objects are rejected).
Raw device evidence / measurement logs are never stored; core neither receives nor persists them.
Device identifiers / serials / OS build fingerprints are never collected; the assessment carries deviceIdentifiersIncluded=false.
Persistent assessment history is forbidden; each assessment is transient and current-process only (no tracking record).
Installed-app / package inventory is never collected in core; app scanning is external Secure Device work and is not performed here.
A redacted room summary is metadata-only and separately gated from content; it is refused in Emergency and in locked lifecycles.
A full room summary reveals content and is admission-gated by posture + protected presentation; content denies when the provider is absent or the room requires basic+.
Content read is admission-gated: at_risk denies; basic+ requirement denies (no provider); ScreenShield/Bunker requirement denies; unverified-only rooms follow other policy.
Content mutation is admission-gated identically to content read; at_risk and unmet posture/protection deny.
Local content search is admission-gated as a content action; at_risk and unmet posture/protection deny.
Content export is not implemented; it is a future gate and is denied after admission gating (no export surface exists in core).
Content copy/clipboard extraction is not implemented in core (clipboard handling is external Secure Device work); denied.
File open is not implemented in core (file_ref objects are references only); denied as a future gate.
Local AI over room content is not implemented (Gate I); denied as a future gate, and additionally admission-gated.
A protected-content intent is a data-only description; core never renders content, blocks screenshots, or claims capture protection; future-required protection denies content.
A sensitive-room session is transient/current-process only; a denied admission cannot create an admitted session; it is never persisted and never survives a restart.
Per-operation session revalidation invalidates on any posture/provider/policy/membership/mode/lifecycle/action change; recovery requires a fresh assessment + admission + authorization.
A trusted provider that could elevate posture (basic/hardened/high_assurance/managed_bunker) requires the SEPARATE Secure Device project + the Secure Device Integration Gate + ADR.
Native ScreenShield is external; a ScreenShield requirement DENIES content now with no silent downgrade and no active-protection claim.
Managed Bunker Session Mode is implemented in the SEPARATE Secure Device project; any Bunker requirement denies content now.
Identity model (invites/verification/recovery) is Gate G work.
QR/short-code verification is designed at Gate G.
The Tauri shell is a placeholder with a zero permission surface until Phase 9 hardening.
